headline: Roderick Paulate recalls Dolphy's advice for him to stay in comedy  GMA News Online
text:
Before Roderick Paulate became one of Philippines' best comedians, he was actually an award-winning dramatic actor.
The "Batang Riles" star recalled on "Fast Talk with Boy Abunda" Friday how Dolphy himself talked him into staying put as a comedy actor.
"Sa set ng 'John And Marsha sa Probinsya,'" Roderick began. "May ginawa akong scene, na lalaki siya na 'Shirley, halika ka nga rito.' Pero yun pala si Rolly 'yung gusto niya puntahan," he recalled.
At that time, Roderick was still winning awards for his dramatic roles. "Pero siya [Dolphy] ang nagsabi, 'tawang-tawa kami sa'yo. 'Lika nga dito anak. Dick, yaw mo mag-comedy? Mag-comedy ka na lang. Ituloy mo na lang."
According to Roderick, the King of Comedy wanted him to stay put and keep on doing comedy. "Kailangan ng bago," he recalled Dolphy as saying.
It seems Roderick heeded Dolphy's advice because after "John and Marsha sa Probinsiya" screened in 1985, he went on to star in hit. comedy movies like "Mga Anak ni Facifica Falayfay," "Gorio and Tekla," "Petrang Kabayo" and sealed his reputation as one of Philippines' best comedians. 
Roderick will star in the upcoming "Mga Batang Riles" which will premiere on GMA Prime on January 6.
— LA, GMA Integrated News

headline: Dozens rescued in South Korea building fire, emergency department says  GMA News Online
text:
SEOUL, South Korea - A fire and thick smoke engulfed an eight-story building in the South Korean city of Seongnam on Friday, sending 12 people to hospital with smoke inhalation, and about 30 people who had been trapped were safely rescued, the fire department said.
The department deployed 82 vehicles and equipment south of Seoul to bring the blaze under control after the fire started in a restaurant on the ground floor of the busy commercial building, which also housed a swimming pool where children take lessons.
Earlier, YTN television reported multiple people were trapped inside, with footage showing black smoke engulfing a large part of the building. — Reuters

headline: World food price index eases in December, pushed lower by sugar  GMA News Online
text:
ROME, Italy - The United Nations' world food price index dipped in December against November levels, led lower by a drop in international sugar quotations, but still showed a robust gain year-on-year, data showed on Friday.
The index, compiled by the U.N. Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) to track the most globally traded food commodities, fell to 127.0 points last month from a slightly revised 127.6 in November.
The November figure was previously put at 127.5.
The December value was up 6.7% from 12 months previously, yet remained 20.7% below the all-time high reached in March 2022, FAO said.
For 2024 as a whole, the index averaged 122.0, 2.1% lower than the 2023 value, offsetting significant decreases in quotations for cereals and sugar with smaller increases in prices for vegetable oils, dairy and meats.
Sugar prices led December's monthly decline, dropping 5.1% month-on-month thanks to improving sugarcane crop prospects in the main producing countries to stand 10.6% below its December 2023 level.
Dairy prices declined after seven consecutive months of increases, losing 0.7% from November but still posting a 17.0% gain year-on-year. Vegetable oil prices dropped 0.5% month-on-month, but were up 33.5% on their year-earlier level.
Meat prices rose 0.4% in December from November and stood 7.1% above their December 2023 value.
The FAO cereal price index was little changed last month from November and was 9.3% below its year-earlier level, as a slightly uptick in maize quotations offset a drop in those for wheat, FAO said.
FAO did not provide a new forecast for global cereal production, with the next estimate due next month. — Reuters

headline: Boy Abunda weighs in on Barbie Forteza and Jak Roberto's breakup  GMA News Online
text:
Boy Abunda gave his two cents' worth on Barbie Forteza and Jak Roberto's breakup.
On Friday's "Fast Talk with Boy Abunda," the Kapuso host read Barbie's statement on the breakup, adding that he reached out to Jak but the actor asked for time before making a comment.
For Tito Boy, any goodbye is painful, especially in the context of a romantic relationship.
"Seven years is a long time, masakit ito pero ang nakakabilib po is pinili ni Barbie to come from a place of love, pinili ni Barbie in that statement na ang aalalahanin ko ay 'yung magagandang mga bagay," he said.
"We shared a lot of things, she's coming from a place of love, hindi lang nag-work, pero masakit man, but they were seven wonderful years. I became who I am today because of you, because of that relationship."
Tito Boy also pointed out the part where Barbie wished for Jak to find the love he deserves and how they must both be in pain.
He likewise refrained from speculating, noting that there are a lot of speculations on social media about why they broke up.
"We don't want to speculate kasi andami-dami po sa social media speculations kung bakit po sila naghiwalay. I think we should be praying na sana'y maging maayos kasi seven years from now, when we look back, we'll be able to connect the dots kaya pala ganun."
The King of Talk added that Barbie choosing to come from a place of maturity and love is admirable.
He also noted the cryptic post of Barbie's older sister Gabrielle which was posted on X on the eve of the breakup announcement, but refused to make further comments.
"I don't wanna go into that because I might be putting meaning into some things that we don't know. Hayaan natin sila mag-heal, but don't get the statement wrong, it is a painful experience," said Tito Boy.
"So, Barbie, you take good care of yourself, maraming salamat for sharing with us your statement. And Jak, mag-ingat kayong dalawa, God bless the both of you."
Barbie announced her split from Jak on Thursday. In her statement, the Sparkle star said that having him in her life was the happiest she had ever been and thanked him for loving her the way he did. They were together for seven years.
A day before the announcement, her sister posted on X a photo of Barbie laughing, with the caption, "Gusto ko happy ka."
Barbie's final post with Jak on Instagram was in December 2024 when they celebrated the actor's 31st birthday.
— CDC, GMA Integrated News

headline: At least 40 contingents to compete in Sinulog Grand Showdown 2025  GMA News Online
text:
At least 40 contingents are set to compete in the “Sinulog Grand Showdown 2025: The Jubilee Year” on January 19, 2025 at the Cebu City Sports Center.
The Sinulog Foundation, Inc. has released the Official List of Contingents for the Sinulog Grand Showdown 2025.
At least 11 are out-of-town contingents, 18 from Cebu City barangays and schools, 11 from Cebu Province, and guest performers from the Cebu Provincial Government, Cebu City Government, and MASSKARA Festival of Bacolod City.
The following is the Official List of Contingents:
Out-of-town contingents
Cebu City/Barangays/Schools
Cebu Province
— GMA Regional TV News

headline: Comelec informs SC of start of ballot printing for Eleksyon 2025  GMA News Online
text:
The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has transmitted a letter to the Supreme Court (SC) informing about the start of the printing of the official ballots for the 2025 national and local elections (NLE). 
Comelec Chairperson George Erwin Garcia said this is to notify the SC of the developments on the preparations for this year’s midterm polls. 
“I am officially writing a letter to our honorable Chief Justice, informing the SC, that we will start the printing of ballots by January 6,” said Garcia.
“It is not to influence the SC on what to do sa mga kaso na nai-file sa kanila subalit para maiwasan 'yung nangyari nung nakaraang halalan na napapagalitan ang Comelec dahil hindi naiinform ang SC kung ano na ang updates sa ating preparations. Ngayon po bawat kilos, bawat ginagawa natin ay reported sa SC,” he said. 
(The intention is not to influence the Supreme Court regarding the cases filed before it, but rather to avoid a repeat of what happened during the previous elections, where the Comelec was reprimanded for not informing the Supreme Court of updates on our preparations. Now, every action we take is reported to the Supreme Court.)
Some 2025 NLE aspirants including former Caloocan representative Edgar Erice sought relief from the High Court regarding the disqualification of their poll bid. 
According to Garcia, individuals with cases declared as final and executory will be excluded from the ballot. However, should they secure a TRO from the SC, the poll body will comply with the ruling and insert their names. 
"May declaration na final and executory ‘yung kaniyang decision and judgement. Ibig sabihin as far as the Comelec is concerned, 'yung may final and executory ay tanggal ang pangalan sa listahan,” Garcia said. 
(The Comelec en banc deemed its decision as final and executory. As far as the Comelec is concerned, aspirants with final and executory decision will be excluded in the ballot.)
“Hanggat wala kaming nakukuhang TRO, we will have to proceed with the printing of ballots,” he said 
(As long as there is no TRO, we’ll have to proceed with the printing.)
The Comelec will start the printing of the 73 million ballots on Monday, January 6, 2025 until April 14, 2025.—AOL, GMA Integrated News

headline: Comelec eyes local absentee voting expansion for Eleksyon 2025  GMA News Online
text:
The Commission on Elections (Comelec) plans to expand the local absentee voting (LAV) system to include more government workers, especially healthcare professionals, for the 2025 national and local elections (NLE).
“Gusto natin ito i-expand. Ibig sabihin mas maraming empleyado ng pamahalaan ang nakakapag-avail ng LAV,” Comelec Chairperson George Garcia told reporters on Friday.
(We aim to expand this initiative to allow more government employees to avail of LAV.)
“Lalo na sa healthcare sector na alam naman natin nakadeploy sila nang mas maaga pag nagkakaroon ng halalan… Sana ‘yung ibang ahensya rin na kalimitan may trabaho na ginagawa na patungkol sa halalan, marapat sana na makagawa agad ng request sa Comelec para makasama ang kanilang opisina sa LAV,” he said.
(Particularly in the healthcare sector, as they are often deployed earlier during elections. We also encourage other government agencies involved in election-related work to submit their requests promptly, so their offices can be included in the LAV program.)
Currently, absentee voters in the Philippines include members of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), the Philippine National Police (PNP), and media practitioners whose duties fall on election day.
The Comelec has not yet announced the registration period for LAV. However, according to the election body’s calendar for the 2025 midterm elections, the absentee voting period is scheduled from April 28 to April 30, 2025.
The midterm elections will take place on May 12, 2025. — DVM, GMA Integrated News

headline: Marcos removes VP, past presidents in NSC reorganization  GMA News Online
text:
President Ferdinand ''Bongbong'' Marcos Jr. has reorganized the National Security Council, removing the Vice President and past presidents in its composition. 
In signing Executive Order No. 81, Marcos said there is a need to further guarantee that NSC remains a resilient national security institution, capable of adapting to evolving challenges and opportunities both here and abroad, and to ensure that NSC members “uphold and protect national security and sovereignty, thereby fostering an environment conducive to effective governance and stability.”
Under the latest Marcos' directive, the NSC will be composed of the following: 
Meanwhile, the executive committee shall be composed of the following: 
Executive Order No. 330 issued on July 1, 1950 created the NSC, according to the Presidential Communications Office.
On the other hand, Executive Order No. 115 issued on December 24, 1986 reorganized the NSC and defined its membership, functions, and authority.
Under former President Gloria Macapagal-Arroyo's Executive Order No. 34, the Vice President was part of the NSC composition.
GMA News Online asked the PCO for reason over the reorganization of the NSC.
In a brief statement, Executive Secretary Lucas Bersamin said at the moment, the Vice President is ''not considered relevant to the responsibilities of membership in the NSC.''
Bersamin said the President, however, has the power to add members to the composition of the NSC as needed.
''Nonetheless, when the need arises, the EO reserves to the President the power to add members or advisers,'' he said.
To recall, the NSC said it considers all threats to Marcos as serious and a matter of national security after Vice President Sara Duterte made a kill remark against the First Couple and Speaker Ferdinand Martin Romualdez.
Duterte, in return, had questioned why as a member of the NSC, she was not invited to the meetings of the council, which National Security Adviser Eduardo Año said is “closely coordinating with law enforcement and intelligence agencies to investigate the nature of the threat, the possible perpetrators, and their motives.” 
GMA News Online sought Duterte for comment but she has yet to reply as of posting time.
The Vice President earlier clarified that her remark against President Marcos, First Lady Liza Araneta Marcos, and Romualdez was not a threat but only highlighting the supposed threat against her security.—AOL, GMA Integrated News
